insight: For the Australian team in the 2026 World Cup, hydration breaks have had a mixed impact on their momentum. In half of their breaks, they managed to gain momentum, with a slight increase in performance, as indicated by a mean change of +0.050 per minute. However, when Australia was pressing before a break, they actually lost momentum, with their performance declining by an average of 0.100 per minute, and they were unable to regain it after the break. In contrast, when they weren't pressing before a break, they were able to capitalize on the pause and come back stronger, with their performance increasing by an average of 0.200 per minute.
